items.title: As semiconductor stocks slump, one AI-adjacent sector is thriving — and these are the stocks to buy there
items.description: Another way to exploit the AI evolution is available for investors.

items.title: Oil prices are up 13% in a week — and could head for more than $100 a barrel
items.description: West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude both saw their largest one-week percentage gains in months.

items.title: I have racked up $35,000 in credit-card debt. Should I file for bankruptcy?
items.description: “I’m also considering working with a credit-counseling agency or enrolling in a hardship program.”

items.title: Airbnb co-founder says his X account was hacked as he deletes comments about tokenization
items.description: Airbnb founder and CEO has deleted comments about tokenization that were posted to his account.

items.title: The market may be headed for a 40% correction, warns this wealth manager. He flags bargains in overlooked stocks.
items.description: Ted Oakley of Oxbow Advisors sees a market that is “not normal,” with investors locked into the S&P 500 that is not giving them a diversified portfolio.

items.title: Ranch dressing and stuffed raccoons: Some of the World Cup’s biggest winners as Spain and Argentina face off in the final
items.description: As the World Cup wraps up, the off-field battle for eyeballs, ad dollars and viral fame has already crowned some champions

items.title: ‘Respect is important’: If my financial adviser said, ‘Hey,’ my wife and I would walk out. Where do you draw the line?
items.description: “I met with a financial representative who was not aware that AT&T was the shortened form of American Telephone and Telegraph.”

items.title: SpaceX postponed a crucial launch — now its stock is set to slide even further below the IPO price
items.description: Shares in SpaceX declined about 4% in premarket trading on Friday after aborted mission.

items.title: Silently freaking out about retirement? Why it may not be as bad as you think.
items.description: Amid all the gloom and doom, there are ways to change the math in your favor.

items.title: Retail investors had been outperforming the market since May. Not any more.
items.description: Retail investors had a pretty strong two-month stretch when they were outperforming the broader market by as much as 10 percentage points.
